hyperedges.label: P4 Background HIBP Breach Re-check Flow
Test Plan / Run Report / Status Documentation Chain
Credential CRUD keeps HIBP prefix store in sync

nodes.label: CSP Drift Guard Pattern
nodes.rationale: vercel.json must match canonical CSP from securityHeaders.ts; parity test fails the build if they diverge, preventing the class of bug where documented policy and deployed policy diverge

nodes.label: PRF-v1 Vault Key Wrapping Scheme
nodes.rationale: Vault key is wrapped with HKDF-SHA256-derived key from WebAuthn PRF output. PRF output never stored — XSS or full IndexedDB dump cannot re-derive wrapping key. Previous device-key scheme was insecure because inputs (credentialId, userId, salt) were all stored and recomputable.

nodes.label: S7 Non-Extractable Session Key
nodes.rationale: unwrapVaultKeyWithPRF takes raw vault key bytes (not a CryptoKey) so the session CryptoKey can stay non-extractable; transient raw bytes zeroized in finally block after import

nodes.label: PBKDF2 600k Iterations OWASP 2025
nodes.rationale: PBKDF2_ITERATIONS=600000 per OWASP 2025 guidelines; deriveKeyFromPassword imports as non-extractable WebCrypto key and zeroizes transient derived bytes afterward (S7)

nodes.label: Self-Hosted OCR Assets Pattern
nodes.rationale: Tesseract assets served from public/ocr/ (self-hosted, same-origin) instead of cdn.jsdelivr.net, eliminating CDN supply chain risk (P2). Assets pinned via package-lock.json and copied by scripts/copy-ocr-assets.js at predev/prebuild.

nodes.label: S4 Rehash-on-Login Pattern
nodes.rationale: Transparently upgrades legacy/weak scrypt password hashes to current parameters on successful login. Best-effort: rehash failure must never block login.

nodes.label: S1 PRF-Only Biometric Invariant
nodes.rationale: Only PRF-scheme (vaultKeyScheme='prf-v1') credentials can unlock vault. stripLegacyBiometric removes legacy credentials on each password login. Enrollment requires WebAuthn PRF extension; if authenticator does not enable PRF, enrollment is rejected.

nodes.label: S8 Schema Validation Before Repository
nodes.rationale: Every vault import validated against Zod schema BEFORE any row touches the repository. Malformed, oversized, or wrong-shaped input rejected with user-friendly error. Unknown keys stripped (not rejected) for forward compatibility with future export versions.

nodes.label: PRF Tri-State Support Detection (unknown/supported/unsupported)
nodes.rationale: Only blocks or warns when PRF is *known* unsupported — not when merely unverifiable — to avoid false negatives on browsers where getClientCapabilities is unavailable

nodes.label: Master Password Zeroized After Enrollment Attempt
nodes.rationale: Never retain the password in state longer than the enrollment attempt — S7 key hygiene applied to transient form state
nodes.norm_label: master password zeroized after enrollment attempt

nodes.label: Supply Chain First Phase Order Rationale
nodes.rationale: Supply chain hardening (A, B) runs first because removing cdn.jsdelivr.net and dead deps shrinks the CSP allowlist, making the strict CSP (C) simpler and testable in one pass. Key hygiene (D) runs alone with integration-test gate.

nodes.label: Apple Touch Icon (180x180) — TrustVault
nodes.description: iOS home-screen icon for TrustVault PWA. Circular dark navy (#1a2035 approx) background. Centre: teal/mint-green shield outline containing bold 'T.V' initials with a small keyhole dot below the letters. Consistent with the app's security/vault branding. 180×180 px, no rounded-corner clipping (iOS applies that itself).

nodes.label: Favicon 32x32 — TrustVault
nodes.description: Browser tab favicon at 32×32 px. Dark navy circular background with the teal shield + 'TV' monogram visible at small size. Same design language as larger icons — readable at browser-tab scale.

nodes.label: Maskable PWA Icon Template SVG
nodes.description: 512x512 maskable PWA icon with full-bleed dark navy background (#1a1a2e). Features a vault shield shape with teal stroke (#16c79a), inner shield detail at 30% opacity, bold 'TV' text in teal at font-size 90, and a lock keyhole detail (circle + rect) at the shield bottom. Content is scaled within the 40% safe zone (307.2px center area). Uses dark navy (#1a1a2e) and deep blue (#0f3460) background tones with teal (#16c79a) accent.

nodes.label: Standard PWA Icon Template SVG
nodes.description: 512x512 standard PWA icon with a circular dark navy background (#1a1a2e, r=240, stroke #0f3460). Features a large vault shield path with teal stroke (#16c79a, stroke-width 12), inner shield detail at 30% opacity, bold 'TV' text in teal at font-size 140, and a lock keyhole detail (circle + rect) at the shield bottom. Designed for display on all backgrounds. Color palette: dark navy (#1a1a2e), deep blue (#0f3460), teal (#16c79a).

nodes.label: PWA App Icon 192x192 — TrustVault
nodes.description: Standard PWA manifest icon at 192×192 px. Dark navy circle containing a teal/mint outlined shield with 'T.V' monogram and a small keyhole/dot detail at the base of the shield. Used by Android Chrome for home-screen shortcuts and splash screens. Color scheme: ~#1a2035 navy background, ~#2de0a5 teal accent.

nodes.label: PWA App Icon 512x512 — TrustVault
nodes.description: High-resolution PWA manifest icon at 512×512 px. Identical design to 192×192 variant but rendered at full resolution — dark navy circular background, double-stroke teal shield, bold 'T.V' initials, and keyhole dot. Used by Chrome for splash screens and app listing imagery. Fine details of the shield double-border clearly visible.

nodes.label: TrustVault Visual Brand Identity
nodes.description: Shared visual identity across all TrustVault icons: dark navy background (~#1a2035), teal/mint accent (~#2de0a5), shield motif representing security/protection, 'T.V' monogram (TrustVault initials), and a keyhole/dot symbol reinforcing the vault/password-manager theme.

nodes.label: PWA App Icon SVG
nodes.description: 512x512 PWA icon with a solid black background (#121212). Features a green circular outline (r=180, stroke #4CAF50, stroke-width 16) resembling a clock face or vault dial, a clock/pointer path from center (256,156) to (256,256) to (326,326) in green, a central green dot (r=16), and 'TrustVault' text in green (#4CAF50, font-size 48) at the bottom. This is an earlier or alternate design using a clock/safe-dial metaphor rather than the shield motif. Color palette: black (#121212), green (#4CAF50).

nodes.label: TrustVault Icon Design System
nodes.description: The visual design language used across TrustVault PWA icons. Primary motifs: vault shield shape and lock keyhole (icon-template, icon-maskable-template) or safe-dial/clock metaphor (pwa-icon). Common themes: dark backgrounds, security-oriented iconography, 'TV' or 'TrustVault' branding. Two color schemes: navy+teal (#1a1a2e, #16c79a) in template icons vs black+green (#121212, #4CAF50) in the original pwa-icon.

nodes.label: PWA Maskable Icon 192x192 — TrustVault
nodes.description: Maskable PWA icon at 192×192 px designed for adaptive icon shapes. Dark navy square background (fills entire canvas to the edges). The teal shield + 'T.V' logo is centred and sized to sit within the 40% safe zone, ensuring no clipping under circular, squircle, or teardrop mask shapes. The navy extends to canvas edges (no circular crop on the asset itself), making it safe-zone compliant.

nodes.label: PWA Maskable Icon 512x512 — TrustVault
nodes.description: High-resolution maskable PWA icon at 512×512 px. Dark navy square background extending edge-to-edge. Teal shield + 'T.V' monogram centred and contained well within the maskable safe zone (~40% of canvas). The circular ring around the shield is visible but the outer navy padding guarantees the focal logo survives any adaptive-icon mask. Safe-zone compliant for Android adaptive icons.
